scene_title: Before the Duke of Albany's palace

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Welcome, my lord: I marvel our mild husband

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Not met us on the way.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Madam, within; but never man so changed.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: I told him of the army that was landed;

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: He smiled at it: I told him you were coming:

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: His answer was “The worse:” of Gloucester’s treachery,

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: And of the loyal service of his son,

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: When I inform’d him, then he call’d me sot,

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: And told me I had turn’d the wrong side out:

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: What most he should dislike seems pleasant to him;

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: What like, offensive.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: To Edmund. Then shall you go no further.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: It is the cowish terror of his spirit,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That dares not undertake: he’ll not feel wrongs

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Which tie him to an answer. Our wishes on the way

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: May prove effects. Back, Edmund, to my brother;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Hasten his musters and conduct his powers:

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: I must change arms at home, and give the distaff

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Into my husband’s hands. This trusty servant

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Shall pass between us: ere long you are like to hear,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: If you dare venture in your own behalf,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: A mistress’s command. Wear this; spare speech;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Decline your head: this kiss, if it durst speak,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Would stretch thy spirits up into the air:

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Conceive, and fare thee well.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Yours in the ranks of death.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: My most dear Gloucester!

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: O, the difference of man and man!

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: To thee a woman’s services are due:

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: My fool usurps my body.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Madam, here comes my lord.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: I have been worth the whistle.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: O Goneril!

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: You are not worth the dust which the rude wind

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Blows in your face. I fear your disposition:

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: That nature, which contemns its origin,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Cannot be border’d certain in itself;

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: She that herself will sliver and disbranch

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: From her material sap, perforce must wither

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: And come to deadly use.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: No more; the text is foolish.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Wisdom and goodness to the vile seem vile:

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Filths savour but themselves. What have you done?

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Tigers, not daughters, what have you perform’d?

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: A father, and a gracious aged man,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Whose reverence even the head-lugg’d bear would lick,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Most barbarous, most degenerate! have you madded.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Could my good brother suffer you to do it?

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: A man, a prince, by him so benefited!

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: If that the heavens do not their visible spirits

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Send quickly down to tame these vile offences,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: It will come,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Humanity must perforce prey on itself,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Like monsters of the deep.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Milk-liver’d man!

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That bear’st a cheek for blows, a head for wrongs;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Who hast not in thy brows an eye discerning

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Thine honour from thy suffering; that not know’st

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Fools do those villains pity who are punish’d

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Ere they have done their mischief. Where’s thy drum?

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: France spreads his banners in our noiseless land;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: With plumed helm thy slayer begins threats;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Whiles thou, a moral fool, sit’st still, and criest

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: “Alack, why does he so?”

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: See thyself, devil!

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Proper deformity seems not in the fiend

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: So horrid as in woman.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: O vain fool!

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Thou changed and self-cover’d thing, for shame,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Be-monster not thy feature. Were’t my fitness

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: To let these hands obey my blood,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: They are apt enough to dislocate and tear

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Thy flesh and bones: howe’er thou art a fiend,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: A woman’s shape doth shield thee.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Marry, your manhood now—

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: What news?

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: O, my good lord, the Duke of Cornwall’s dead:

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Slain by his servant, going to put out

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: The other eye of Gloucester.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Gloucester’s eye!

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: A servant that he bred, thrill’d with remorse,

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Opposed against the act, bending his sword

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: To his great master; who, thereat enraged,

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Flew on him, and amongst them fell’d him dead;

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: But not without that harmful stroke, which since

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Hath pluck’d him after.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: This shows you are above,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: You justicers, that these our nether crimes

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: So speedily can venge! But, O poor Gloucester!

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Lost he his other eye?

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Both, both, my lord.

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: This letter, madam, craves a speedy answer;

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: ’Tis from your sister.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: One way I like this well;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: But being widow, and my Gloucester with her,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: May all the building in my fancy pluck

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Upon my hateful life: another way,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: The news is not so tart.—I’ll read, and answer.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Where was his son when they did take his eyes?

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Come with my lady hither.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: He is not here.

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: No, my good lord; I met him back again.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Knows he the wickedness?

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Ay, my good lord; ’twas he inform’d against him;

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: And quit the house on purpose, that their punishment

lines.speaker: MESSENGER
lines.text: Might have the freer course.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Gloucester, I live

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: To thank thee for the love thou show’dst the king,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: And to revenge thine eyes. Come hither, friend:

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Tell me what more thou know’st.

glosses.definition: gentle husband — Goneril's contempt for Albany
bear led by the head — a dangerous animal
transformed and disguised — Goneril has put on a fiend's skin over her own

glosses.phrase: O, the difference of man and man!
glosses.definition: Goneril's contempt for Albany vs. Edmund
a token Goneril gives Edmund — possibly a ring or chain
my fool of a husband possesses me — Goneril's bitter line about Albany
creature covered by (her own) fiendishness disguised as woman

annotations.title: Albany's transformation — from silence to denunciation
annotations.body: Albany in 4.2 is no longer the cautious husband of 1.4. He calls Goneril 'most barbarous, most degenerate,' predicts 'humanity must perforce prey on itself / Like monsters of the deep,' and compares her to a fiend 'self-cover'd' in a woman's skin. The rhetoric is a transformation: he has seen what his wife is capable of and is speaking it. The scene also delivers the news of Cornwall's death (from the servant's wound in 3.7) by report. Goneril's aside — 'Our fears in Cornwall have weighed against my prayers' — shows the sisters calculating power: with Cornwall dead, Regan is free to claim Edmund, and the rivalry that will destroy them both is now active.
sources: Jonathan Bate, Soul of the Age (2008)

annotations.title: Albany's 'monsters of the deep' — the play's cannibalism image
annotations.body: Albany's 'Humanity must perforce prey on itself, / Like monsters of the deep' is the play's most compressed image of moral collapse. The earth's creatures consume each other by appetite; what distinguishes humanity is restraint. When Goneril and Regan act from pure will, without restraint, humanity stops being distinguishable from predation. The image has a Hobbesian premonition — Hobbes's 'bellum omnium contra omnes' is half a century away but the thought is legible here. Shakespeare is also gesturing at specific Jacobean anxieties: the post-Gunpowder-Plot fear of social dissolution. Albany speaks for the slow-developing conscience of the play, and his rhetoric here is the play's most explicit warning that moral order is frailer than anyone had assumed.

trials.prompt: How has Albany changed between 1.4 and 4.2?
options: From cautious silence to open moral denunciation — 'most barbarous, most degenerate' — the play's conscience-figure steps into speech
That humanity is defined by restraint against appetite; when Goneril and Regan act from pure will, humanity ceases to be distinguishable from predation
That ocean travel should be avoided
